company_type: Insurance Brokerage and Risk Management Services

dependencies: RISX-FACS proprietary risk management information system for claims administration
Third-party vendors for technology and cloud services
Substantial operations in India providing client support and services
Underwriting enterprises for insurance placement capacity
risks: Disruption to RISX-FACS system could materially affect claims administration operations
Dependence on third-party cloud services and vendors for IT infrastructure
Concentration of operations in India exposed to geopolitical risks (India-Pakistan Kashmir dispute, India-China tensions, terrorism)
Reliance on underwriting enterprises for risk-taking capital capacity
Integration risks from acquisitions affecting technology systems

regulatory_risks: SEC, NYSE, DOJ, IRS, FTC, FINRA, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network regulations in the U.S.
Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) regulations in the U.K.
Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) regulations
Insurance regulators in nearly every jurisdiction of operation
Pension law and financial regulation in multiple countries
Cybersecurity and data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, state-level privacy laws)
AI regulations (EU AI Act, emerging U.S. and global AI laws)
Climate-related disclosure regulations (CSRD, ESRS, TCFD, ISSB, California climate disclosure rules)
FCPA and U.K. Bribery Act 2010 anti-corruption compliance
FATCA (Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act) requirements
International sanctions laws (U.S., U.K., EU)
OECD Pillar 2 global minimum corporate tax (15% rate)
Bermuda corporate income tax regime (effective 2025)
State restrictions on non-compete agreements (Minnesota, North Dakota, Oklahoma, California, FTC ban)
IRS investigation of IRC 831(b) micro-captive advisory services (promoter investigation since 2013)
Compensation method regulations for insurance brokers
Licensing requirements across multiple jurisdictions
Employment and labor relations laws globally
Competition and antitrust laws
Currency and foreign exchange regulations
China blocking statute requiring compliance with Chinese laws conflicting with U.S. laws
Increased scrutiny of offshore labor centers (India operations)
DOJ Corporate Criminal Enforcement Policies on personal devices and third-party messaging apps
New York State Department of Financial Services Cybersecurity Regulation
Sustainability and ESG disclosure requirements (varying by jurisdiction)
Anti-ESG backlash and related legal risks

sanction_risks: Armed conflicts in Ukraine and Middle East increasing sanctions laws and compliance complexity
Heightened cybersecurity attack risks due to Ukraine and Middle East conflicts
U.S., U.K., and EU sanctions laws restricting trade with designated governments and parties
Risk of violations of sanctions laws by employees or third parties in international operations
Potential for asset freezing under sanctions regimes
New sanctions laws resulting from armed conflicts affecting business operations
Compliance with conflicting sanctions laws across jurisdictions (U.S., U.K., EU)
Reputational and financial penalties for sanctions violations
